summary: First-pass smoke test of FocusRead backend + frontend. Backend endpoints healthy; all frontend flows (text, URL, controls, actions) work end-to-end. The test URL in the request (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bionic_reading) does not exist on Wikipedia (upstream 404) — not a backend bug; verified extraction with an existing URL returned 39k words correctly.
passed: POST /api/extract-url with valid URL (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reading) -> 200, title='Reading - Wikipedia', word_count=39056, source_url present, non-empty text
POST /api/extract-url with empty URL -> 400 {detail: 'URL is required'}
POST /api/extract-url with unresolvable domain -> 400 with RequestException detail
Frontend loads at / with hero, InputPanel (4 tabs), ControlPanel, BionicReader empty-state (screenshot verified)
input-text-sample-btn + input-text-apply-btn -> 74 .bionic-b spans render in reader-article; header shows '68 words · 1 min'
control-fixation-strong grows bold portions (sum 56 -> 71 across first 20 spans) — reader re-renders
control-theme-toggle toggles <html> class '' -> 'dark' (dark mode screenshot confirmed)
control-font-trigger: 'Spectral · Serif' -> 'JetBrains · Mono' after selecting Mono option
URL flow: filling input-url-field with a real Wikipedia URL + clicking input-url-fetch-btn renders 39,061 bionic bold spans in reader (integration backend->frontend works)
reader-copy-btn click succeeds and 'Text copied to clipboard' toast appears
reader-clear-btn empties reader (returns to 'AWAITING INPUT' empty state, header shows '0 words · 1 min')
notes: The requested test URL https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bionic_reading returns 404 from Wikipedia itself (verified via curl -I -L). Backend correctly surfaces this as 400 with the underlying 404 message. For a positive URL test, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reading (or /wiki/Bionic_reading_(technique)) should be used instead.
PDF and Image (OCR) flows were not exercised with real files per instructions; UI tabs and dropzone controls are visually present in the InputPanel.
Empty-state hero contains ~9 demo .bionic-b spans by design — this is not stale post-clear content; reader header shows 0 words after clear.
action_items: Optional: update the sample/documented test URL from /wiki/Bionic_reading (404 upstream) to /wiki/Bionic_reading_(technique) or another existing article, so QA scripts don't hit a false-negative.
